column1: - Vue files with `class` attributes in `<template>` caused ts-morph parsing errors because the entire `.vue` file was passed as TypeScript
- Now only the `<script>` block content is extracted via regex and parsed, matching the approach used by the [community fix](https://github.com/badihi/arkit/commit/335e27a)
- While ts-morph v27 improved tolerance for Vue files, proper `<script>` extraction is the semantically correct approach
- [x] Added `test/vue-sample/` fixture with `App.vue` and `Hello.vue` containing `class` attributes
- [x] Added parser test verifying Vue files parse correctly and imports resolve

Vue files with class attributes in templates caused ts-morph parsing
errors because the entire file was passed as TypeScript. Now only the
<script> block content is extracted and parsed.

- Adds CommonJS export detection (`module.exports = ...` and `exports.foo = ...`) as a fallback when ts-morph finds no ES6 exports
- Fixes #946 — Node.js microservices using `module.exports` now show exports in architecture diagrams
- Documents the issue-fixing workflow in CLAUDE.md for future maintenance
- `src/parser.ts`: Added `EXPORTS_RE` regex and CommonJS fallback in `getExports()` method
- `test/commonjs-sample/`: New test fixture with `module.exports`, `exports.*`, and `require()` patterns
- `test/parser.test.ts`: New test case verifying CommonJS export detection
- Updated snapshots (Express test now detects CommonJS exports)
- [x] New `CommonJS module.exports and exports.* detected` test passes
- [x] All 18 snapshots pass (5 updated for new CommonJS detection)
🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)","fix: detect CommonJS module.exports and exports.* patterns (#946)
The parser only detected ES6 `export` keyword via ts-morph. CommonJS
patterns like `module.exports = {}` and `exports.foo = fn` were invisible,
causing Node.js microservices using CommonJS to show no exports in diagrams.
Added EXPORTS_RE regex fallback in getExports() that scans source text when
ts-morph finds no exports. Also documents the issue-fixing workflow in
+    const parser = new Parser(new Config({ directory }))
+    // helper.js should have named exports detected
+    // app.js should import both helper and index
